title: Thelypteris sichuanensis
taxonomy: Thelypteris sichuanensis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is native to China. It is classified within the genus Thelypteris. The genus Thelypteris is part of the order Polypodiales. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or the wood ferns.

intro: Thelypteris sichuanensis, also known as the Sichuan maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to China. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris sichuanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Thelypteris sichuanensis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of capsules and tablets.
